reply_context.text: Even in a parliamentary case where centralized vote counting is explicitly happening: this is actually just the stop-condition of the collective decision making process. reply_context.text: Without accounting for network topology, you can't reason about communication rate, which is why the mean/median alternatives the authors consider here seem appropriate. posts.text: Without accounting for network topology, you can't reason about communication rate, which is why the mean/median alternatives the authors consider here seem appropriate. But they are ultimately hand-waving away where the "collective intelligence" actually *happens*: the deliberation process. 4/

reply_context.text: Aggregation rules need to account for network topology. The aggregation rules here presume something like a flat linear topology (where everyone is connected to a single vote tallier) or a fully connected clique (where everyone is mutually connected and can communicate instantaneously). 3/

reply_context.text: In biological systems like swarms, consensus formation is a dynamical process. Consensus is a fixed point, an evolutionary stable state arrived at via convergence. Any particular agent in a population only has instantaneous access to a local subset of information provided by immediate neighbors. 2/

x'=Rot(x)
z,z'=f(x),f(x')
c,c'=g(z,y),g(z',y)
L=c+c'

what I'm suggesting:
L=c+c' +dist(z,z')

I strongly suspect adding a term like this to your objective would accelerate training.

posts.text: This created a dangerous situation for me where I began to allow my valuable novel ideas to drift into domains that definitely bordered on a kind of mania. reply_context.text: The consequence was that I was having legitimately interesting conversations with the LLM where it was helping me clarify genuine and valuable novelty, but which was intermixed with nonsense that neither it nor I was well equipped to recognize as such.

posts.text: The consequence was that I was having legitimately interesting conversations with the LLM where it was helping me clarify genuine and valuable novelty, but which was intermixed with nonsense that neither it nor I was well equipped to recognize as such.
